mnemonics: FIVE Resources For A Solid Blockchain FOUNDATION: F (Foundational Papers), I (Interactive Demos), V (Video Lectures), E (Ethereum Docs), F (Forums & Communities)
keyPoints: Foundational academic papers (e.g., Nakamoto's Bitcoin whitepaper) provide the original theoretical framework.
Interactive platforms like Remix IDE for smart contracts or Blockchain Demo offer hands-on learning.
Official documentation, especially for major platforms like Ethereum, is a critical primary source.
commonMistakes: Assuming all learning resources are equally reliable; prioritize official docs and academic papers over informal blogs.
Only using one type of resource (e.g., only videos); a mix of theory, practice, and community discussion is best.
Getting lost in advanced resources before grasping the core concepts of cryptography and distributed consensus.
